title: GB to MB Converter
shortDescription: Convert gigabytes to megabytes using the binary storage ratio of 1 GB = 1024 MB.
metaDescription: Convert gigabytes to megabytes with the 1 GB = 1024 MB ratio for file sizes, storage planning, and device capacity.
intro: Enter a gigabyte value and get the equivalent in megabytes using the binary ratio of 1 GB = 1024 MB.
overview:
This converter is designed for storage figures that follow the binary convention, where gigabytes are converted to megabytes by multiplying by 1024. It is useful when you need to interpret device capacity, backup sizes, software packages, or folder totals shown in GB.

If a specification, dashboard, or system readout gives you a value in gigabytes, this tool gives the matching megabyte amount without manual calculation. It is especially helpful when you need to compare several sizes on the same unit scale or document values in technical notes.
howItWorks: Type the number of gigabytes you want to convert.
The tool multiplies that value by 1024 to produce megabytes.
Read the result and continue with related storage conversions if needed.

faq.question: Why does 1 GB equal 1024 MB here?
faq.answer: This tool follows the binary storage convention used in many computing contexts, where each step between units is based on 1024.

faq.question: What happens if I enter a decimal value?
faq.answer: Decimal values are accepted and converted normally. For example, 1.5 GB becomes 1536 MB.

faq.question: Does this tool show decimal-based storage units too?
faq.answer: No. It is limited to the binary GB to MB conversion, so 1 GB is treated as 1024 MB rather than 1000 MB.

faq.question: What is a common mistake when using this converter?
faq.answer: A common mistake is mixing binary storage units with decimal marketing figures. If a source uses 1000-based labeling, the result will differ from this calculator.

faq.question: Can I use the result for exact quota checks?
faq.answer: Yes, if the quota is also defined in binary units. If the limit is stated by a provider using decimal units, confirm the unit system before comparing.
